post.description: OAuth2 compatible token login, get an access token for future requests.

get.description: Get current user.

Retrieve a document by ID for citation reverse lookup.

This endpoint allows clients to resolve [[doc:<document_id>]] citations
from chat completions. It returns the document metadata and content preview.

Requires authentication and workspace access authorization.

Args:
    document_id: The unique document identifier from a citation
    workspace_id: Required workspace ID for access validation
    user: Authenticated user context

Returns:
    DocumentCitationResponse with metadata and content preview

Raises:
    HTTPException: If user lacks workspace access or document not found

post.description:
OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions API with RAG integration.

Supports:
- Workspace resolution via model name (karag:<workspace_name> or karag:<workspace>:<mode>)
- Mode extraction from model name or system messages
- Automatic RAG retrieval with citation support
- Streaming and non-streaming responses
- OpenAI-compliant error format

Requires authentication and workspace access authorization.

Citation format in response: [[doc:<document_id>]]

get.description:
List available models (workspaces) in OpenAI-compatible format.

Each workspace is exposed as a model in the format: karag:<workspace_name>
This allows OpenAI SDK clients to discover available workspaces.

Only returns workspaces the authenticated user has access to.

Args:
    user: Authenticated user context

Returns:
    ModelsResponse with list of accessible workspaces as models

Get a specific model (workspace) by ID.

Requires authentication and workspace access authorization.

Args:
    model_id: The model ID in format karag:<workspace_name>
    user: Authenticated user context

Returns:
    ModelInfo for the specified workspace if user has access

Raises:
    HTTPException: If user lacks workspace access or model not found

post.description:
Create a new API key for a workspace.

SECURITY: This is the ONLY time the full key is returned.
Store it securely - it cannot be retrieved later.

Required Permission: admin

get.description:
List API keys for a workspace.

Note: Returns metadata only, NEVER the full key.

Required Permission: admin

delete.description:
Revoke an API key.

Required Permission: admin

put.description:

Some changes may require a system restart.

Required Permission: admin

get.description:
Get workspace configuration.

Required Permission: admin

put.description:
Update workspace configuration.

Changes are clamped to system limits.

Required Permission: admin

get.description:
Query usage logs with filters.

Required Permission: admin

Get usage statistics for a workspace.

Required Permission: admin
Get chat history for a specific thread.
List chat threads with pagination support.

patch.description:
Update the title of a chat thread.

Args:
    thread_id: The unique identifier of the chat thread
    payload: Contains the new title
    workspace: Current workspace context

Returns:
    AppResponse confirming the update

get.description: Get a specific chat thread by ID.

delete.description:
Delete a chat thread.

Args:
    thread_id: The unique identifier of the chat thread
    workspace: Current workspace context

Returns:
    AppResponse confirming the deletion

post.description:
Stream chat updates using Server-Sent Events (SSE).

This endpoint streams chat responses in real-time and spawns
background title generation for a consistent UX.

Args:
    payload: Chat stream request containing message and configuration
    workspace: Current workspace context

Returns:
    StreamingResponse with text/event-stream content type

Example:
    Client connects and receives SSE events:
    - data: {"type": "token", "content": "Hello"}
    - data: {"type": "token", "content": " world"}
    - data: {"type": "done"}

delete.description: Delete a dataset from the workspace.

APIKey.title: APIKey
APIKey.description:
API Key for workspace-scoped authentication.

ISOLATION: Each key maps to exactly one workspace.

Security:
- Keys are hashed with Argon2, only prefix is stored
- Full key is shown ONLY once on creation
- Keys can be revoked with audit trail

Usage:
- Header: X-API-Key: <key>
- Or: Authorization: Bearer <key>

warning.title: Warning
warning.default: This is the ONLY time the API key will be displayed. Store it securely.

APIKeyCreateResponse.title: APIKeyCreateResponse
APIKeyCreateResponse.description: Response when creating a new API key - ONLY time full key is visible.

AppResponse.title: AppResponse
AppResponse.description:
Standardized API response wrapper.

All API responses follow this structure for consistency:
{
    "success": bool,
    "code": str,
    "message": str,
    "data": T | null
}

Type Parameters:
    T: The type of data contained in the response

Examples:
    >>> # Success response with data
    >>> response = AppResponse.success_response(
    ...     data={"id": 1, "name": "Test"},
    ...     message="User created successfully"
    ... )

    >>> # Error response
    >>> response = AppResponse.business_failure(
    ...     code="VALIDATION_ERROR",
    ...     message="Invalid input provided"
    ... )

model.title: Model
model.description: ID of the model to use (format: karag:<workspace>[:mode])

stream.title: Stream
stream.description: Whether to stream back partial progress

max_tokens.title: Max Tokens
max_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate

ChatCompletionRequest.title: ChatCompletionRequest
ChatCompletionRequest.description:
OpenAI-compatible chat completion request schema.

Model format: karag:<workspace_name> or karag:<workspace_name>:<mode>
- mode can be: chat, qa, tutor, strict_rag

role.title: Role
role.description: The role of the message sender (user or assistant)

sources.title: Sources
sources.description: Source documents used for the response

thread_id.title: Thread Id
thread_id.description: The ID of the chat thread

Dataset.title: Dataset
Dataset.description:
Dataset - logical storage unit representing chunks & docs.
Replaces "document storage" to follow standard naming.

DocumentCitationResponse.title: DocumentCitationResponse
DocumentCitationResponse.description:
Response for citation document lookup.

Used when frontend resolves [[doc:<document_id>]] from chat responses.

DocumentListItem.title: DocumentListItem
DocumentListItem.description: Alias for DocumentResponse for consistency in lists.

DocumentResponse.title: DocumentResponse
DocumentResponse.description:
Standard document response for frontend integration.

This schema is used for:
- Document listing in workspace storage
- Citation click-through from chat
- Document metadata display

role.title: Role
role.description: The role of the message author (system, user, assistant, tool)

tool_calls.title: Tool Calls
tool_calls.description: Tool calls made by the assistant

tool_call_id.title: Tool Call Id
tool_call_id.description: Tool call ID for tool messages

PipelineConfig.title: PipelineConfig
PipelineConfig.description: Complete pipeline configuration for a workspace.

RAGConfig.title: RAGConfig
RAGConfig.description:
RAG configuration - controls retrieval behavior.

MUTABILITY: All fields are runtime mutable.
Changes take effect immediately for new requests.

RAGTrace.title: RAGTrace
RAGTrace.description: Detailed RAG operation trace for observability.

RateLimitConfig.title: RateLimitConfig
RateLimitConfig.description:
Rate limiting configuration (non-billing).

These limits prevent abuse and ensure fair resource usage.

pdf_reader.title: Pdf Reader
pdf_reader.description: PDF reader to use (e.g. pymupdf, unstructured)

RetrievedSource.title: RetrievedSource
RetrievedSource.description: Individual retrieved chunk source for RAG trace.

SearchEngineConfig.title: SearchEngineConfig
SearchEngineConfig.description: Configuration for the search engine pipeline layer.

SystemConfig.title: SystemConfig
SystemConfig.description:
System-level configuration (operator-only).

MUTABILITY:
- Runtime mutable: allowed_models, request_timeout
- Restart required: max_context_window, embedding_dim

ACCESS: Operator/admin only, not visible to workspaces.

status.title: Status
status.description: Current status (pending, processing, completed, failed, canceled)

TaskMetadata.title: TaskMetadata
TaskMetadata.description: Flexible metadata for different task types.

UsageLog.title: UsageLog
UsageLog.description:
Usage log entry for observability.

REDACTION: No API keys, tokens, or PII in logs.
All sensitive fields are hashed or omitted.

STORAGE: Written to MongoDB (usage_logs collection) and/or
exported to external log aggregation.

id.title: Id
id.description: Short unique ID of the workspace

WorkspaceConfig.title: WorkspaceConfig
WorkspaceConfig.description:
Workspace-level configuration (restricted).

Workspaces can modify these within system-defined limits.

id.title: Id
id.description: Short unique ID of the workspace

WorkspaceUsageStats.title: WorkspaceUsageStats
WorkspaceUsageStats.description: Aggregated usage statistics for a workspace.
